title: ‘The Marvels’ Is Here. Is It Too Late for the MCU?
format: Joanna Robinson joins to talk through The Marvels, the box-office disappointment and the post-Endgame wobble it caps off, then Sean sits down with Kristoffer Borgli on Dream Scenario.

segments.heading: ‘The Marvels’
segments.note: with Joanna Robinson, author of MCU: The Reign of Marvel Studios

films.title: The Marvels
films.note: Co-written and directed by Nia DaCosta, the sequel to Captain Marvel. Delayed from summer to November after Quantumania's reception, then visibly recut around Iman Vellani's Kamala Khan. Sean grades it a B-minus to C-plus; Joanna a B; Amanda says she genuinely couldn't follow much of the plot. Opened to $46.1 million domestic, the lowest of any MCU film.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: The billion-dollar 2019 original this is a sequel to, directed by Anna Boden and Ryan Fleck. Amanda remembers almost nothing about it beyond Annette Bening and a villainous Jude Law.

films.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
films.note: Its poor February reception is what Joanna says triggered the aggressive recutting of The Marvels.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: Cited as a recent MCU movie less visibly hacked up in post.

films.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
films.note: Also cited as less mangled than The Marvels, and referenced again below for its Charles Xavier and Mister Fantastic cameos.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Sean's line for the floor: he doesn't think The Marvels is worse than this, which he calls an objectively bad movie.

films.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp
films.note: The other movie Sean ranks below The Marvels, calling it 'aggressively fine.'

films.title: Iron Man
films.note: Discussed at length via Joanna's book: Favreau, Robert Downey Jr., and Gwyneth Paltrow essentially improvising the film into existence on a loose outline, the opposite of the current 'hackathon in post' method.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
films.note: Sean's counterpoint on weak villains: Lee Pace's Ronan, in a cowl that barely let him act, was worse than Dar-Benn.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: The box-office benchmark The Marvels is being compared against for a second-weekend hold.

films.title: Five Nights at Freddy's
films.note: Cited for its own steep second-weekend drop, despite opening to nearly double The Marvels' number.

films.title: Fast X
films.note: Amanda notices the same mismatched-eyebrow continuity issue in Brie Larson's close-ups here as she spotted in this film.

segments.heading: The stinger, the X-Men, and 2024
segments.note: Kelsey Grammer's Beast, Deadpool & Wolverine, and next year's slate

films.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
films.note: The only 2024 MCU release. Expected to be a nostalgia tour through Fox's X-Men properties, with Hugh Jackman returning as Wolverine and Jennifer Garner rumored as Elektra.

films.title: X-Men: First Class
films.note: Sean's pick for a top-three X-Men movie: 'just hot X-Men.'

films.title: Logan
films.note: Sean's other top-three X-Men pick.

films.title: X2
films.note: Rounds out Sean's top three X-Men movies.

films.title: Star Trek: First Contact
films.note: Joanna's favorite Star Trek film, offered unprompted mid-tangent about the franchise's influence on Marvel's current cosmic era.

films.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
films.note: Joanna's number two; Sean argues it should be number one.

films.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
films.note: Joanna's number three, the Christopher Plummer entry.

films.title: Venom: The Last Dance
films.note: One of three Sony superhero movies on next year's five-film comic-book slate.

films.title: Kraven the Hunter
films.note: Aaron Taylor-Johnson as the Spider-Man villain, directed by J.C. Chandor. Sean, a Chandor fan, says the trailer looks dreadful.

films.title: Madame Web
films.note: Dakota Johnson in the title role, with Sydney Sweeney playing a character the hosts expect to eventually become Spider-Woman.

films.title: Joker: Folie à Deux
films.note: The lone DC entry on the 2024 comic-book slate, and one of Sean's most anticipated films of the year, sight unseen.

films.title: Superman
films.note: James Gunn's DC relaunch, name-checked as still on track despite the 2025 release date.

segments.heading: In conversation
segments.note: growing up in Norway, dream logic, and working with Nicolas Cage

films.title: Dream Scenario
films.note: A24 film starring Nicolas Cage as a mild-mannered professor who begins inexplicably appearing in strangers' dreams. Cage told Borgli he related to the script and handed him creative control; Borgli calls it a genuine collaboration rather than deference to a star.

films.title: Sick of Myself
films.note: Borgli's prior Norwegian film, funded through European grants, about factitious-disorder narcissism. He calls the lead character's arc 'chillingly American' despite the Norwegian setting.

films.title: Whateverist
films.note: His breakout short film, a semi-autobiographical comedy about a failed musician rationalizing quitting, made while Borgli worried he couldn't call himself a director.

films.title: Drib
films.note: An obscure hybrid mockumentary-reenactment about a real Norwegian energy-drink ad campaign gone wrong. Borgli admits he invented most of the underlying story to get Norwegian Film Institute funding, and confesses on air that he lied about it to the Norwegian press for years.

films.title: Happiness
films.note: The last great thing Borgli says he's seen: a rare 35mm rewatch at the New Beverly, the film he credits with making him obsessed with Dylan Baker, who appears in Dream Scenario.

films.title: Anatomy of a Fall
films.note: Its packed, enthusiastic Beyond Fest crowd is what got Borgli excited to screen Dream Scenario for the same audience a week later.
